tool.name: Metabolic Flux Enrichment Analysis

param.name: type_selector
param.label: Input format:
param.option: Fluxes of group 1 + Fluxes of group 2 + ... + Fluxes of group N

param.name: input_name_fluxes
param.label: Dataset's name:
param.help: Default: Dataset

param.name: controlgroup
param.label: Control group label:
param.help: Name of group label to be compared to others

param.name: choice
param.label: Use advanced options?
param.help: Use this options to choose custom parameters for evaluation: statistical test, pValue, Fold-Change threshold and specify output maps.

param.name: test
param.label: Statistical test to use
param.help: Select the statistical test to use for the analysis based on your data and the amount of replicates. Paired T-test and Wilcoxon need the same number of replicates in each group.

param.name: pValue
param.label: P-value threshold:
param.help: min value 0

param.name: adjusted
param.label: Use adjusted p-values
param.help: Should the program adjust the P-values based on the FDR (Benjamini-Hochberg) correction?

param.name: fChange
param.label: Fold-Change threshold:
param.help: min value 1

param.name: generateSvg
param.label: Generate SVG map
param.help: should the program generate an editable svg map of the processes?

param.name: generatePdf
param.label: Generate PDF map
param.help: should the program return a non editable (but displayble) pdf map of the processes?

param.name: controlgroup
param.label: Control group label:
param.help: Name of group label to be compared to others

param.name: choice
param.label: Use advanced options?
param.help: Use this options to choose custom parameters for evaluation: statistical test, pValue, Fold-Change threshold and specify output maps.

param.name: test
param.label: Statistical test to use
param.help: Select the statistical test to use for the analysis based on your data and the amount of replicates. Paired T-test and Wilcoxon need the same number of replicates in each group.

param.name: pValue
param.label: P-value threshold:
param.help: min value 0

param.name: adjusted
param.label: Use adjusted p-values
param.help: Should the program adjust the P-values based on the FDR (Benjamini-Hochberg) correction?

param.name: fChange
param.label: Fold-Change threshold:
param.help: min value 1

param.name: generateSvg
param.label: Generate SVG map
param.help: should the program generate an editable svg map of the processes?

param.name: generatePdf
param.label: Generate PDF map
param.help: should the program return a non editable (but displayble) pdf map of the processes?

param.name: user_fluxes_file
param.label: Pre-computed enrichment file
param.help: TSV file with columns: ids, P_Value, fold change, z-score, average_1, average_2. This is the tabular result produced by a previous FluxToMap run.

param.name: pValue
param.label: Maximum P-value to display:
param.help: Reactions with P-value above this threshold will be shown as dashed grey arrows. The value is applied directly to the P_Value column in the file.

param.name: fChange
param.label: Minimum |fold change| to display (normalized, range 0–1):
param.help: This threshold is applied DIRECTLY to the 'fold change' values in the file, which must be in the normalized range (-1, 1) computed as (avg1 - avg2) / (|avg1| + |avg2|). Reactions with |fold change| below this value will be shown as grey (non-significant) arrows. This is NOT a standard ratio-based fold change threshold.

param.name: generateSvg
param.label: Generate SVG map
param.help: Should the program generate an editable SVG map?

param.name: generatePdf
param.label: Generate PDF map
param.help: Should the program return a non editable PDF map?

tool.help:
What it does -------------
This tool analyzes and visualizes differences in reactions fluxes of groups of samples, returned by the Flux Simulation tool, of groups of samples.
Accepted files are (Files must have different names): - option 1) two or more fluxes datasets, each referring to samples in a given group. The user can specify a label for each group; - option 2) one fluxes dataset and one group-file specifying the group each sample belongs to (e.g. the accepted group file is thought to be the one returned by the Clustering tool); - option 3) a pre-computed enrichment file (tabular result from a previous FluxToMap run), allowing the user to skip all computation and directly color the map.
Optional files: - custom svg map. Graphical elements must have the same IDs of reactions. See HmrCore svg map for an example.
The tool generates: - A tab-separated file: reporting fold-change and p-values of fluxes between a pair of conditions/classes; - A metabolic map file (downloadable as .svg and .png): visualizing up- and down-regulated reactions between a pair of conditions/classes; - Two metabolic maps to visualize the mean and median fluxes normalized amongst all classes. The associated color map is also generated (absolute ranges); - A log file (.txt).
Output options: - To calculate P-Values and Fold-Changes and to enrich maps, comparisons are performed for each possible pair of groups (default option 'One vs One').
Alternative options are: - comparison of each group vs. the rest of samples (option 'One vs Rest') - comparison of each group vs. a control group (option 'One vs Control). If this option is selected the user must indicate the control group label.
Output files will be named as classA_vs_classB. Reactions will conventionally be reported as up-regulated (down-regulated) if they are significantly more (less) active in class having label "classA".
Example input -------------
"Fluxes of group 1 + Fluxes of group 2 + ... + Fluxes of group N" option:
Fluxes Dataset 1:
+------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | Reaction ID| TCGAA62670 | TCGAA62671 | TCGAA62672 | +============+================+================+================+ | r1642 | 0.523167 | 0.371355 | 0.925661 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1643 | 0.568765 | 0.765567 | 0.456789 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1640 | 0.876545 | 0.768933 | 0.987654 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1641 | 0.456788 | 0.876543 | 0.876542 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1646 | 0.876543 | 0.786543 | 0.897654 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+
Fluxes Dataset 2:
+------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | Reaction ID| TCGAA62670 | TCGAA62671 | TCGAA62672 | +============+================+================+================+ | r1642 | 0.523167 | 0.371355 | 0.925661 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1643 | 0.568765 | 0.765567 | 0.456789 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1640 | 0.876545 | 0.768933 | 0.987654 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1641 | 0.456788 | 0.876543 | 0.876542 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1646 | 0.876543 | 0.786543 | 0.897654 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+
"Fluxes of all samples + sample group specification" option:
Fluxes Dataset:
+------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | Reaction ID| TCGAA62670 | TCGAA62671 | TCGAA62672 | +============+================+================+================+ | r1642 | 0.523167 | 0.371355 | 0.925661 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1643 | 0.568765 | 0.765567 | 0.456789 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1640 | 0.876545 | 0.768933 | 0.987654 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1641 | 0.456788 | 0.876543 | 0.876542 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+ | r1646 | 0.876543 | 0.786543 | 0.897654 | +------------+----------------+----------------+----------------+
Group-file
+---------------+-----------+ | Patient ID | Class | +===============+===========+ | TCGAAA3529 | MSI | +---------------+-----------+ | TCGAA62671 | MSS | +---------------+-----------+ | TCGAA62672 | MSI | +---------------+-----------+
Pre-computed enrichment file - column description --------------------------------------------------
When uploading a pre-computed enrichment file the tool skips all dataset loading and statistical computation entirely. The values in the file are used directly to style the metabolic map.
**The only controls available to the user are the two filtering thresholds:**
- **P-value threshold**: reactions with a P-value above this threshold are shown as dashed grey arrows (not significant), regardless of their fold change. The value is applied directly to the ``P_Value`` column in your file.
- **Fold-change threshold**: reactions with ``|fold change|`` below this threshold are shown as solid grey arrows (below threshold). The value is applied directly to the ``fold change`` column in your file. Since fold change values in this file must already be in the normalized range (-1, 1), the threshold you provide must also be in that range (e.g. 0.2 means only reactions with |fold change| >= 0.2 will be coloured). This is NOT the same as the standard fold-change threshold used in normal computation mode (where the user provides a value >= 1 that is internally converted).
The file must be tab-separated and contain exactly these columns:
+----------+---------+-------------+---------+-----------+-----------+ | ids | P_Value | fold change | z-score | average_1 | average_2 | +==========+=========+=============+=========+===========+===========+ | r1642 | 0.032 | 0.45 | 1.23 | 0.52 | 0.31 | +----------+---------+-------------+---------+-----------+-----------+ | r1643 | 0.071 | -0.21 | -0.87 | 0.41 | 0.53 | +----------+---------+-------------+---------+-----------+-----------+
**ids**: reaction identifier as encoded in the metabolic map (e.g. "GPI", "TALDO1"). Must match the IDs present in the selected map exactly.
**z-score**: z-score of the difference between the two group means, used to scale the width of the arrows on the map. Higher absolute z-scores result in thicker arrows.
**average_1**: mean flux value of the first group or condition being compared. This is the mean across all samples belonging to the first group for a given reaction.
**average_2**: mean flux value of the second group or condition being compared. This is the mean across all samples belonging to the second group for a given reaction. A reaction is considered up-regulated when average_1 > average_2 and down-regulated when average_1 < average_2.
**TIP**: If the user provides just one dataset for analysis: -Use the Cluster Analysis tool to assign group labels when no prior division information is available -provide an external group file specifying the assignment of each sample, if the group division is known a priori
